title: Plugin Manifest
description: Schema for eve-plugin.yaml manifests covering both provider and package plugin kinds.

api_version.description: API version. Only eve.plugin/v1 is accepted; unknown versions must be refused.

kind.description: Plugin kind determines the required commands and allowed fields.

display_name.description: Human-readable plugin name shown in the TUI and CLI listings.

commands.description: Map of command name to execution spec. Required commands differ by kind.

supports.description: Compatibility constraints. Shape differs by kind.

env.description: List of environment variable names this plugin reads.

actions.description: UI actions exposed by provider and package plugins.

capabilities.description: Behavioral flags the UI/core can consult. Distinct from supports (compatibility) and actions (user-clickable).

compatibility.description: Compatibility matrix listing supported platform/desktop/session combinations (package plugins only).

compatibility_enforced.description: Whether compatibility failures are hard errors during dispatch (package plugins only).

bootstrap.description: Provider-owned first-contact bootstrap hints. sudo_password_env names which of this provider's config/secret env vars holds the password core uses to install NOPASSWD sudo (provider plugins only); core reads it generically and never names a provider.

access.description: Provider-owned VM access identity rules used to populate resolved access.bootstrap_user, provision_user, and human_user.

conflicts_with.description: Package ids that cannot coexist with this package (package plugins only).

provides.description: Capability tokens this package offers to the resolved bundle (package plugins only). Tokens are abstract '<facet>:<value>' strings (e.g. 'session:wayland', 'capture:unattended') — never package ids — so any package can negotiate compatibility without naming another package. Core aggregates these across the bundle.

requires_capabilities.description: Capability tokens ('<facet>:<value>') that must be provided by the rest of the resolved bundle for this package to function. Dispatch fails clearly if any is unmet (v4.4 §8).

conflicts_capabilities.description: Capability tokens ('<facet>:<value>') that disable this package when present in the resolved bundle. Replaces core-side, package-named compatibility guards (v4.4 §8).

vagrant.description: Vagrant-engine contributions from this package (package plugins only). A package names only its own ports — self-contained, not a package-name leak.

config_schema.description: Config and secret field declarations. Valid on both provider and package manifests. On packages, values are namespaced under the package id (e.g. mypkg.my_field).

down.description: Package down behavior metadata (package plugins only).

update.description: Per-OS-family shell command (run on the instance over SSH) to upgrade this package to its latest version (package plugins only). Aggregated and run by scripts/update-tools, so core names no package.

desktop.description: Desktop metadata a desktop package contributes (package plugins only). Drives desktop/session detection in core data-driven, without naming packages: core reads the selected desktop package's name/session instead of branching on ids.

headless.description: Whether this is a headless variant (preferred when multiple desktops are selected).

install.description: Install metadata per OS family, consumed by provisioning scripts (package plugins only).

depends_on.description: Package IDs this plugin requires. Used by the resolver to validate that dependencies are selected and to order install steps so dependencies run first.

catalog.description: Provider-contributed catalog rows (machines, provider-specific OS image fields, inits). Only meaningful on provider manifests. The aggregator merges these onto shared OS identity rows to produce the effective catalog.

env_emission.description: Provider-declared env-var emission rules. Core evaluates the union of all provider entries against each resolved instance to produce the provider-specific portion of the resolved env, keeping core free of provider id literals.

host_tools.description: Host-side CLI tools this provider requires. ``doctor`` reads these generically to check tool availability.

bundles.description: Bundle definitions contributed by this package manifest. Each bundle composes a set of included package ids. Only meaningful on package manifests.

artifacts.description: Declared build outputs for this package. Phase 0: declaration only — no execution wiring.

requires.description: Compatibility requirements. The eve range is enforced at load as a core gate (reject if the running core version is outside the range). The plugins map is validated for shape only; full graph resolution is deferred to Phase 1.

eve.description: Semver range string for the required eve core version (e.g. ">=4.0,<5.0" or "^4.0").

plugins.description: Map of plugin id to required semver range string.

command_spec.description: Execution spec for a single plugin command.

exec.description: Path to the executable, relative to the plugin directory or the project root.

access_rules.description: Access identity rules for one OS family.

access_user_rule.description: Rule for resolving one user identity. env reads an environment variable, value is a constant, location reads a provider location field, and fallback references another access field.

package_supports.description: Package plugin compatibility constraints. Contract rule: the same package id may exist in multiple sources iff their supports are disjoint (e.g. one for ubuntu, one for windows). Overlapping supports for one id is a conflict and requires explicit EVE_PLUGIN_ALLOW_OVERRIDE. Enforcement is deferred to the Phase 1/3 resolver; today load_all already rejects duplicate kind:id pairs without that override.

action.description: A UI action exposed by the plugin.

label.description: Button label shown in the TUI.

target.description: Action target dispatched by package-action, provider dispatch, or a compatibility Make target.

exec.description: Launcher command path (relative to the plugin dir) that core execs generically for this action. When set, core resolves the plugin dir, exports the shared EVE_REMOTE_* context + profile env, and runs it — no target string is interpreted by core. Enables package-owned remote launchers (v4.4 §8).

wait_for.description: Another action target to run (and succeed) before this action. Used by launchers that need a service ready first, replacing core-side wait hooks.

destructive.description: Whether the UI must confirm before running this action.

expected_output.description: Expected output shape for callers that need to parse the action result.

help.description: Short operator-facing help text for the action.

interactive.description: Whether this action needs an attached terminal/browser-capable interactive session.

native.description: Whether this action uses a native protocol handler.

requires.description: Optional state predicates that control when this action is available.

action_prompt.description: A user-choice prompt rendered before the action fires.

env.description: Environment variable name for the selected choice.

prompt_choice.description: A single choice in an action prompt.

compatibility_entry.description: One row in the package compatibility matrix.

field_spec.description: A single config or secret field declared by a provider manifest.

env_var.description: Environment variable name(s) CoreEmit for this field. For config fields, ConfigEnv emits the value during config-env emission (make/shell/json). For secrets, provider dispatch also injects the value at runtime. A field may map to multiple env vars by giving a list.

catalog_contribution.description: Provider catalog contribution block: machines, provider-specific OS image/compatibility rows, inits, and locations.

catalog_location.description: A location row contributed by a provider manifest. Field-merged by name across providers so each provider contributes its own per-location data (region, host, etc.).

host_tool_entry.description: One host-side CLI tool a provider requires (checked by ``doctor``).

check.description: Shell command to verify the tool is installed.

from.description: Source ref(s) into the resolved context, tried in order (coalesce — first non-empty wins). Refs: os.<field>, location.<field>, provider_config.<field>, machine.defaults.<field>, env:<ENV_VAR>.

default.description: Value emitted when all sources are empty/null.

match_provider.description: Only emit the derived value when the declaring provider IS the instance's provider; otherwise emit empty string.

catalog_machine.description: A machine row contributed by a provider manifest, mirroring config/catalog.yaml machine shapes.

catalog_os.description: A provider-specific OS image/compatibility contribution. Only the provider's own image fields are declared here (e.g. an AMI pattern, a cloud image URL, a metal image); core names none — every provider-specific selector flows through additionalProperties, while shared identity (family/version/arch) is merged by the aggregator.

id.description: OS identifier matching a shared OS identity row.

catalog_init.description: An init row contributed by a provider manifest, mirroring config/catalog.yaml init shapes.

bundle_entry.description: A bundle composes a set of included package ids.

artifact_entry.description: A declared build output for a package. Phase 0: declaration only — no execution wiring.

download.description: Action target for downloading this artifact.
